# contextlake

> A local context layer for your AI tools: mirror your Git repositories, index them into a
> queryable knowledge graph + wiki, and serve it to AI editors over MCP. Offline-first, so
> agents answer from real source instead of guessing. Python CLI, published on PyPI.

## Docs
- [Overview](https://sayak.in/contextlake/docs.html): What contextlake is and the Mirror, Knowledge, Serve model.
- [Quickstart](https://sayak.in/contextlake/quickstart.html): Install, bootstrap, and wire your editor in a few minutes.
- [Usage & configuration](https://sayak.in/contextlake/usage.html): Mirror-layer commands, config, branch safety, scheduling.
- [Knowledge layer](https://sayak.in/contextlake/knowledge-layer.html): Build a queryable graph + wiki and serve it over MCP.
- [Architecture & internals](https://sayak.in/contextlake/internals.html): Store, concurrency, extraction, the offline boundary.
- [Changelog](https://sayak.in/contextlake/changelog.html): Release history.

## Source
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/sayak-sarkar/contextlake)
- [PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/contextlake/)
